nodes.x: THEN answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
How long [will it be ere] ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.
Wherefore are we counted as beasts, [and] reputed vile in your sight?
He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place?
Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.

BY JOHN JAQUE8.
Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine. The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him. His confidence shall be rooted out of bis tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.—Bildad.
In the fear of the Lord is strong confidence, and his children shall have a place of…
The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.
The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
The gin shall take [him] by the heel, [and] the robber shall prevail against him.
The snare [is] laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.

nodes.lb: Commentary: Kitzur Ba'al HaTurim on Genesis 3:15:1
nodes.x: "עקב" (heel) - both this interpretation and the other one [that עקב means "because"] are valid. [The phrase] "He will bruise your head" emphasizes [God's] punishment [of the serpent], and "you will bruise his heel" refers to the serpent's treachery. This is why our sages said [in reference to the verse in Job 18:10]: "The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way." This…
Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.
be afraid --rather [MAURER], "nor make others to be afraid."
His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction [shall be] ready at his side.
It shall devour the strength of his skin: [even] the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.
His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.
And though he hide himself amongst the nations, Even from thence shall he be rooted out ;
It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because [it is] none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.

As a tree often represents a man, so a branch signifies one aspect of a man (Mal. 4:1; Job 29:19; Prov. 11:28; see also John 15:1-6). Therefore, figuratively, a man has roots, leaves, and branches. Concerning the final status of the wicked, it is said that "his roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off" (Job 18:16). (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Donald W. Parry, A…
His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street.
they shall perish from the earth, and from under these heavens.
He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.
He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.
They that come after [him] shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted.
Surely such [are] the dwellings of the wicked, and this [is] the place [of him that] knoweth not God.
